ocr.text: In the assessment of hemodynamic parameters within the patient cohort, a detailed analysis of various manometric metrics was
conducted to elucidate the relationships between positive and negative pressure readings. The data table encapsulates critical
measurements such as the HP positive and HP negative parameters, which are essential for understanding the pressure dynamics
experienced by patients in the study. Notably, the findings indicate an average HP positive reading of 35, accompanied by an
HP negative measurement of 111. These metrics highlight significant variations in arterial pressure, which may have

implications for patient management strategies.

Furthermore, the mean values for both the TP and FP, alongside their respective
standard deviations, offer valuable insights into the variability of pressure readings within the population. For instance, the TP
mean recorded at 4.1 (with a standard deviation of 2.68) suggests a notable range in patient responses, warranting further
investigation into underlying causes. Collectively, these parameters provide a comprehensive overview of the hemodynamic
status of the patients, facilitating targeted interventions and enhancing clinical outcomes.
